title: A Woman Between Friends

jokes.text: I quit sucking air through my teeth.
jokes.setup: Alex explains his tooth hurts when he drinks cold things or sucks air through his teeth
jokes.punchline: His solution is to stop sucking air rather than see a dentist
jokes.explanation: Clean, economical joke that shows Alex's avoidance behavior. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I'm just afraid of any man who likes to dig and pick in someone's mouth with sharp instruments, that's all.
jokes.setup: Bobby asks if Alex is afraid of dentists
jokes.punchline: Alex reframes dentistry in the most ominous way possible
jokes.explanation: Excellent reframing joke that makes dentistry sound like torture. Strong character-driven humor with quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: Don't tell me when until it's just time to go. I want just enough time to get there and not enough time to think about it.
jokes.setup: Alex agrees to let Bobby make a dentist appointment
jokes.punchline: His elaborate strategy for avoiding anxiety
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's neuroses, though a bit wordy for the payoff.

jokes.text: Do you know that you're acting like a child? / Oh, yeah, and tell him I want to be treated like a child.
jokes.setup: Bobby calls out Alex's childish behavior
jokes.punchline: Alex embraces it and wants the dentist to treat him like a child too
jokes.explanation: Nice turn on Bobby's criticism - Alex owns his childishness completely.

jokes.text: Uh, I'm a boxer... My reactions are quicker.
jokes.setup: Tony claims he saw the woman first
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is that boxers have quicker reactions
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific logic. Tony's boxing background used cleverly for romantic competition.

jokes.text: Hey, Tony, I'm an actor. I'm trained to observe people closely.
jokes.setup: Bobby claims he notices details about women because he's an actor
jokes.punchline: Sets up the test that will deflate his claim
jokes.explanation: Sets up the observation test nicely. Good character work for Bobby's actor pretensions.

jokes.text: What color is Tony's shirt? / I don't know. / Alex's shirt? / I don't know. / The buttons on her coat? / Brown with tan marbling and rose-colored thread.
jokes.setup: Testing Bobby's observational skills on things that should be easy
jokes.punchline: He can't identify his friends' shirts but knows intimate details of the woman's coat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection. The specificity of the button description is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You don't pay attention to women... You, uh, collect them.
jokes.setup: Bobby claims he pays attention to women
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sharp distinction between attention and collection
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical burn from Elaine. The verb 'collect' perfectly captures Bobby's approach.

jokes.text: How many women have you dated this year? / Who counts? / You. Fifteen.
jokes.setup: Bobby tries to deflect the question about how many women he's dated
jokes.punchline: Elaine reveals he literally keeps count
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Bobby's 'who counts?' gets perfectly deflated by the revelation that he does.

jokes.text: When? / Soon as I can get to a telephone.
jokes.setup: Tony asks when Bobby broke up with Cathy
jokes.punchline: Bobby reveals he hasn't actually done it yet
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Bobby's casual attitude toward relationships. Shows his character efficiently.

jokes.text: How come you can't pass up one woman for a friend? / Hormones, I guess.
jokes.setup: Tony appeals to friendship
jokes.punchline: Bobby reduces it to biology
jokes.explanation: Simple but honest. Bobby's blunt honesty about his motivations.

jokes.text: You know, Elaine's right... Make it two out of three.
jokes.setup: Elaine criticizes using a coin flip to decide who asks Janet out
jokes.punchline: Bobby agrees but only wants better odds, not to abandon the idea
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection. Audience expects Bobby to agree with Elaine's moral point, but he just wants better gambling odds.

jokes.text: There aren't that many women that like either one of us, let alone both of us.
jokes.setup: Alex warns about what happens if both get dates with Janet
jokes.punchline: Bobby's realistic assessment of their romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about their limited appeal. Good character moment for Bobby.

jokes.text: Okay, let's agree that we both go out with her, and whoever hits it off best with her gets to keep her.
jokes.setup: They're trying to figure out how to handle both dating Janet
jokes.punchline: Bobby uses language like Janet is an object to be won
jokes.explanation: Shows Bobby's obliviousness to how he talks about women. Cringe but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm thinking of a number... Five. / I wanted five. / Tough luck. / Why don't you draw straws to see who gets five?
jokes.setup: Alex tries to randomly pick who calls the coin flip
jokes.punchline: Both want the same number, leading to Alex's exasperated suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the ridiculous decision-making process. Alex's frustration comes through.

jokes.text: Hey, lady! Anything he says... me, too.
jokes.setup: Bobby is successfully talking to Janet with his smooth lines
jokes.punchline: Tony desperately interrupts with this pathetic attempt
jokes.explanation: Pure desperation from Tony. The physical comedy comes from his interruption and the awkwardness.

jokes.text: A bonus is something that a boss gives a worker when he thinks he's doing a good job. / Congratulations.
jokes.setup: Louie explains what a bonus is to Latka
jokes.punchline: Louie's 'congratulations' as if explaining it IS the bonus
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie manipulation. The setup makes Latka expect something good, then Louie delivers nothing.

jokes.text: Elaine, can I speak to you man to man?
jokes.setup: Tony wants to have a serious conversation with Elaine
jokes.punchline: He uses the wrong phrase for talking to a woman
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism that fits Tony's character. Nothing special but works.

jokes.text: Gee, the guy's got the market cornered on everything but pitiful.
jokes.setup: Tony lists all of Bobby's good qualities
jokes.punchline: Tony realizes Bobby has everything except the quality Tony excels at
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware line from Tony. He finds the one category where he might have an advantage.

jokes.text: Um, I think there's more than nine numbers between those two.
jokes.setup: Tony asks where he ranks on a 1-10 scale with Louie as 1 and Robert Redford as 10
jokes.punchline: Elaine suggests the scale isn't adequate for that range
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical way to insult Louie while being diplomatic to Tony.

jokes.text: Well, I got that beat, 'cause my date with her was one of the greatest nights of my life.
jokes.setup: Bobby says his date was one of the greatest nights of his whole life
jokes.punchline: Tony tries to one-up him but just repeats the same claim
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's competitive nature and lack of verbal skills. He can't actually top Bobby's claim.

jokes.text: Nya, nya, nya, nya, nya.
jokes.setup: Tony and Bobby are fighting over who gets to call Janet
jokes.punchline: Tony resorts to childish taunting
jokes.explanation: Purely childish behavior that fits the escalating immaturity, but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Tony, give it to me, and don't make me do something I'm going to regret... Like hitting myself and getting killed.
jokes.setup: Bobby threatens Tony during their phone fight
jokes.punchline: His threat involves hurting himself instead of Tony
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd threat that perfectly captures Bobby's non-violent nature and sets up the self-hitting gag.

jokes.text: Look. Louie likes it.
jokes.setup: Alex is pointing out how bad their fighting has gotten
jokes.punchline: The fact that Louie enjoys it proves how wrong it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of Louie as a moral compass - if he likes it, it must be wrong. Very economical.

jokes.text: I just remembered, she's not home.
jokes.setup: Bobby magnanimously gives up the phone to Tony
jokes.punchline: He reveals he can't call anyway
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bobby's apparent nobility. Shows he's being strategic, not generous.

jokes.text: He's saying you're stupid.
jokes.setup: Bobby is trying to diplomatically suggest Tony isn't right for Janet
jokes.punchline: Louie cuts through the diplomacy to the brutal truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - he enjoys stirring up trouble by being blunt about what everyone's thinking.

jokes.text: He's a boxer like you're an actor.
jokes.setup: Alex warns Tony he's a boxer so he shouldn't hit Bobby
jokes.punchline: Louie deflates both their professional pretensions at once
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - one line that destroys both characters' self-images. Classic Louie cynicism.

jokes.text: O-O-Okay, okay, listen, uh... I'll hit myself.
jokes.setup: Tony insists on hitting Bobby for calling him stupid
jokes.punchline: Bobby offers to hit himself instead
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd solution that pays off the earlier threat. Shows Bobby's complete non-violence taken to logical extreme.

jokes.text: Not hard enough.
jokes.setup: Bobby hits himself lightly
jokes.punchline: Tony critiques the force of Bobby's self-punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurdity. Tony becomes a director of Bobby's self-abuse.

jokes.text: That's harder than you've ever hit anybody.
jokes.setup: Bobby hits himself harder
jokes.punchline: Louie points out Bobby's violence limitation
jokes.explanation: Good character observation from Louie about Bobby's gentle nature, delivered at the perfect moment.

jokes.text: What am I doing? Breaking up a fight between one guy.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to break up the 'fight' between Bobby and himself
jokes.punchline: Alex realizes the logical absurdity of the situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-observation about the absurdity. Alex steps back and realizes how ridiculous it all is.

jokes.text: You know who I feel sorry for? That poor young girl. Torn between two losers.
jokes.setup: The whole conflict over Janet
jokes.punchline: Louie reframes it as a no-win situation for her
jokes.explanation: Good perspective flip from Louie. Shows his cynicism while making a valid point.

jokes.text: Tony.
jokes.setup: Bobby asks what Janet wanted when she called
jokes.punchline: Alex's one-word answer deflates Bobby completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy. One word that completely destroys Bobby's hopes and expectations.

jokes.text: Oh, well, that's easy to remember; it's the same name. / It's the same girl.
jokes.setup: Latka notes that Tony and Bobby's girlfriends have the same name
jokes.punchline: Bobby reveals it's actually the same person
jokes.explanation: Good use of Latka's innocence to set up the reveal. His logical assumption gets corrected.

jokes.text: Well, could I meet her?
jokes.setup: Latka learns about the shared girlfriend situation
jokes.punchline: His innocent interest in meeting her
jokes.explanation: Shows Latka's innocence and potential interest in joining the competition, which is mildly threatening.

jokes.text: Mine!
jokes.setup: Bobby starts to ask to borrow more money
jokes.punchline: Alex's emphatic refusal before Bobby can even finish
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Alex's frustration with funding their competition. Shows his breaking point.

jokes.text: Oh, what beautiful eyes she has... / Yeah, we had that talk.
jokes.setup: They're listing what they talk about with Janet
jokes.punchline: They've had identical superficial conversations
jokes.explanation: Shows how unoriginal and shallow both their relationships with Janet are.

jokes.text: She calls me her, uh... Sorry, Bobby... Her tough teddy bear. / Her, uh, smart teddy bear.
jokes.setup: They're sharing what Janet calls them
jokes.punchline: Both get teddy bear nicknames that reflect their supposed strengths
jokes.explanation: Good parallel that shows Janet is playing to each one's ego while keeping the basic dynamic the same.

jokes.text: Yeah. I haven't seen anything like it since I stopped reading Archie comic books.
jokes.setup: Elaine comments on the complexity of the relationship
jokes.punchline: She compares it to the Archie/Betty/Veronica love triangle
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that nails the juvenile nature of the situation. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Careful, Nardo. If he gets mad he could beat himself silly.
jokes.setup: Elaine is criticizing Bobby
jokes.punchline: Louie references Bobby's earlier self-hitting with this warning
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that gets funnier because it's presented as a genuine warning about Bobby's 'fighting ability.'

jokes.text: You have a dentist appointment in 30 minutes.
jokes.setup: Alex refuses to get involved and says he can't go anyway
jokes.punchline: Elaine reveals the reason - his dreaded dentist appointment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening subplot. Alex's escape from one problem leads him into another.

jokes.text: Ooh, there they are... tweedle-dumb and tweedle-dumber.
jokes.setup: Tony and Bobby arrive at the bar
jokes.punchline: Louie's variation on the classic Tweedledum and Tweedledee
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits Louie's character. The escalation from 'dumb' to 'dumber' works well.

jokes.text: Why, Land-o-Goshen, it's Mary Worth.
jokes.setup: Alex arrives to mediate the situation
jokes.punchline: Louie compares him to the advice-giving comic strip character
jokes.explanation: Good reference to the meddling advice columnist character. Shows Louie's comic strip knowledge.

jokes.text: Dump the both of them.
jokes.setup: Alex is about to explain the situation to Janet
jokes.punchline: Louie cuts in with his blunt advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - direct, cynical, and probably the smartest advice anyone gives in the episode.

jokes.text: Dump the both of them.
jokes.setup: Louie approaches Janet
jokes.punchline: His blunt advice to dump both Tony and Bobby
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie interference delivered with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Gee, thanks, Janet, but you shouldn't have done this. / Why not? I wanted to. / I'm a middleweight. / And it's my watch.
jokes.setup: Janet gives Tony an engraved watch calling him the next heavyweight champ
jokes.punchline: Tony's corrections reveal multiple problems with the gesture
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how wrong Janet got everything. Multiple layers of error in one gesture.

jokes.text: What situation? / Well, I mean, the fact you're going out with Bobby and Tony at the same time. / Why not?
jokes.setup: Alex assumes Janet knows there's a problem with dating both men
jokes.punchline: Janet genuinely doesn't see any issue with the arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - Janet's innocent 'Why not?' shows she's either very naive or very calculating. Subverts expectations about her awareness.

jokes.text: I'm beginning to wish that I went to the dentist.
jokes.setup: The situation with Janet is getting more complicated
jokes.punchline: Alex would rather face his greatest fear than continue with this
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows how bad the situation has gotten - Alex would choose his worst fear over this.

jokes.text: Yeah, take a minute, mull it over. / Yeah. Come on, now, take your time. / Go over to another table and work on it for a while. / You know, make it look tough, anyway.
jokes.setup: Janet is about to choose between them immediately
jokes.punchline: Both men suddenly want her to take more time and make it look difficult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - they wanted a decision but panic when it comes too easily. Shows their insecurity and need for ego protection.

jokes.text: Yeah, take a minute, mull it over. / Yeah. Come on, now, take your time. / I mean, 'cause we're in no hurry. / Yeah, yeah. Go over to another table and work on it for a while. / You know, make it look tough, anyway.
jokes.setup: They ask Janet to take time to decide
jokes.punchline: Their escalating requests reveal they want her to fake difficulty in choosing
jokes.explanation: Shows their desperation and vanity - they can't bear to be quickly dismissed

jokes.text: How am I doing? / Will you shut up!
jokes.setup: Tony and Bobby are having a heartfelt moment of reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Tony ruins it by asking for feedback
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the sentimental moment. Shows Tony can't help but be self-conscious.

jokes.text: Our friendship could end forever right here. / Eh, maybe you got a point, Bobby. / So let me understand this: Now, neither one of you wants to see me anymore?
jokes.setup: Bobby realizes their friendship is more important than the competition
jokes.punchline: Janet points out they've now both rejected her without her input
jokes.explanation: Great irony - in trying to preserve their friendship, they've made a decision for Janet without consulting her. Shows the selfishness behind their apparent nobility.

jokes.text: Maybe, maybe by telling you that I was going to see to it that the guy I chose had the time of his life tonight. The kind of night that men dream about. The kind of night that... that would be so good that it'd make a man real happy that he's a man.
jokes.setup: Janet wants to get back at Bobby and Tony for rejecting her
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly elaborate description of what the chosen man would have gotten
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and torture for the guys. Janet really knows how to twist the knife.

jokes.text: The person that I was going to pick... Hey, it doesn't make any difference... was Bobby.
jokes.setup: Janet starts to reveal who she would have chosen
jokes.punchline: She pretends it doesn't matter then delivers the crushing news
jokes.explanation: Perfect twist of the knife. Janet's fake nonchalance makes the revelation more devastating.

jokes.text: My tooth stopped hurting.
jokes.setup: Alex needs to leave for his dentist appointment immediately
jokes.punchline: He conveniently discovers he doesn't need to go
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Alex's avoidance. His tooth 'miraculously' heals when confronted with the appointment.

jokes.text: I don't know how you did that, but if you are that determined not to go, well, forget it.
jokes.setup: Alex drinks cold soda with no reaction despite his 'bad tooth'
jokes.punchline: Elaine gives up trying to force him to face his fears
jokes.explanation: Decent resolution to the subplot. Elaine acknowledges Alex's psychological victory over his tooth.

tmdb_overview: Bobby and Tony compete for the same woman.
insight: Friendship tension drives 56 jokes across a character-heavy episode—Taxi's emotional core shines.
